project.name: TextToCAE_BulletPlate_Penetration
project.prompt: Simulate a 7.62 mm bullet penetrating an armor steel plate with Abaqus/Explicit.

connection.message: Abaqus solved the bullet penetration model and extracted ODB results.

material.plate: Armor steel Johnson-Cook plasticity with ductile damage

loads.description: Initial projectile velocity normal to the clamped steel plate

value.en: 150 x 150 x 8 mm clamped armor steel plate; 7.62 mm bullet
value.zh: 150 x 150 x 8 mm 四边固支装甲钢板；7.62 mm 弹体

value.en: Johnson-Cook armor steel plate with rigid equivalent projectile
value.zh: Johnson-Cook 装甲钢板，刚体等效弹头

value.en: Plate edges clamped; projectile initial speed 830 m/s
value.zh: 钢板四边固支；弹体初速 830 m/s

value.en: 240 Abaqus field intervals; browser preview exports 21 sampled dynamic frames
value.zh: Abaqus 场输出 240 个间隔；浏览器预览导出 21 个抽样动态帧

workflow.detail: The penetration request is mapped to bullet geometry, plate geometry, explicit dynamics, contact, initial velocity, and high-frame ODB output.
The Abaqus script creates the 3D plate, projectile, Johnson-Cook steel material, clamped boundary, contact, and explicit job.
Abaqus/Explicit completed the penetration event and wrote the ODB result database.
The exporter extracts a browser-ready transient surface preview with stress contours and projectile motion.
